description: Backtest scenarios for k8s-sage rules engine. ALL expected values are hand-calculated from the constants in internal/rules/recommendations.go and internal/rules/patterns.go. Do NOT derive expected values by running the code.

scenarios.name: Steady — low utilisation (10%)
scenarios.description: Web server requesting 1000m but using ~100m. CV=(120-100)/100=0.20 < 0.30 → Steady.

scenarios.name: Steady — large deployment, very low CV
Burstable — irregular spikes (lower confidence short window)
Burstable — high baseline with small spikes
Burstable — very spiky but not batch (P99/P50 < 5)
Burstable — small request with big spikes
Batch — exactly at spike ratio thresholds
Batch — P50/req exactly at idle boundary (0.05)
Batch — moderate peak with low baseline
Batch — high baseline, extreme max spike
Batch — long-running consistent (borders steady but spike ratios push to batch)
Batch — P50/req=0.06 ensuring not idle, with high spikes
Idle — long data window (7d+), very confident
Idle — insufficient data window (33h < 48h) → falls through to pattern shape
Edge — all negative values (should clamp to zero)
Edge — zero P50 with P95 (high CV edge case)
Edge — very small values (near CPU floor)
Edge — data window exactly at 7d boundary
Edge — negative request (should produce no recommendation)
